site.title: Useful Tools
site.tagline: Free tools that run right in your browser. Your files never leave your device.

home.heading: Tools
home.subheading: Pick a tool and use it instantly.

qr.title: QR Code Generator
qr.description: Turn any link or text into a QR code and save it as PNG or SVG.
qr.emptyHint: Enter a URL or text above to see your QR code.
qr.ecHint: Higher levels survive more damage but make a denser code.
qr.lowContrast: Foreground/background contrast is too low — the code may not scan.
qr.invertedColors: Light foreground on a dark background may fail on some scanners.
qr.renderFailed: Could not generate the QR code. Please check your input.
qr.copyFailed: Copy isn’t supported in this browser. Use download instead.
qr.tooLong: Text is too long to fit in a QR code. Shorten it or lower the error-correction level.
qr.metaTitle: Free QR Code Generator — No Sign-Up, PNG & SVG
qr.metaDescription: Turn any URL or text into a QR code and download it as PNG or SVG. Free, no sign-up, generated right in your browser — nothing you type is sent to a server. Adjust error correction, size, and colors.
howTo: Enter the URL or text you want to turn into a QR code.
Adjust the error-correction level, size, margin, or colors if you like.
Download it as a PNG or SVG, or copy the image.
features: Free with no sign-up — the codes you create never expire.
Save as PNG or vector SVG (SVG is best for print).
Everything runs in your browser — nothing you type is sent to a server.

faq.a: No. You can create and download QR codes right away, with no sign-up or login.
No. These are static QR codes — the data lives inside the image itself, so they keep working forever.
Yes. Both PNG and SVG are free to download. For print, SVG is recommended because it scales to any size without losing quality.

faq.q: Is my data sent to a server?
faq.a: No. The QR code is generated in your browser, and the text or URL you enter is never sent to a server.

faq.q: Can I use it for print?
faq.a: Yes. Download an SVG so it stays sharp at any size. For printing, aim for at least 2×2 cm and 300 DPI.
